SUMMARY:Ensemble Sangineto - Special Online Concert
DESCRIPTION:A special online concert from this eclectic band from Italy:  E
 nsemble Sangineto skillfully blends ancient harmonies and sonorities with 
 modern rhythms.  The delicate combination of the pure and magic tones of t
 he harp and of the bowed psaltery with the guitar intermingle with the thr
 ee voice-polyphonic texture evoking dreamlike and airy atmospheres\,  sim
 ultaneously joyful and light. (A ticket allows viewing of the concert pre
 miere at 7pm Saturday May 21\, or viewing anytime  starting before 7pm Tue
 sday. May 24. Once you begin\, you may continue or rewatch for 24 hours wi
 thin this time period)

 ck only)for $24 general admission\, $22 member/senior/student\, and $10 yo
 uth (ages 6-17). Susan Werner is one of the country’s most compelling li
 ve performers. With formidable chops on guitar and piano (she was a guest

  songs are noted for their poetry and sharp wit\, and also for their aston

DESCRIPTION:One of Scotland’s premier traditional bands! Their diverse re
 pertoire spans the centuries with fire-driven instrumentals\, topical song
 s\, and original ballads and lullabies. The band’s music demonstrates to
  old and young alike the rich and varied musical heritage of the Celtic pe
 ople. (The in-person show is now sold out. Tickets allowing watching durin
 g the livestream\, or anytime starting before 7pm Tuesday are still availa
 ble. Once you begin viewing\, there is a 24 hour window to continue or re-

 church/
SUMMARY:Roy Zimmerman - live at the Keystone Church
DESCRIPTION:A special evening with with premier political satirist Roy Zimm
 erman\, featuring songs from his new release This Machine: The Best of Roy
  Zimmerman. The songs have been chosen from the last 25 years of recordin
 g and touring\,  that best address this critical moment and the current a
 ssault on democratic values.  (The in-person concert takes place Saturday\
 , November 12\, starting at 7:30 pm. Tickets for online views allow joinin
 g during the livestream\, or starting your viewing anytime before 7pm Tues
 day evening. Once you begin viewing you then have 24 hours to continue wat
 ching or re-watch within this window. )

 ir-white/
SUMMARY:Loud Weather -  Elias Alexander & Alasdair White
DESCRIPTION:Fiddle and Bagpipes at play\, with two of North America’s for
 emost exponents of Scottish music. Loud Weather is the inventive musical d
 uo of Fiddle player\, Alasdair White (Battlefield Band\, Dàimh\, Alan K
 elly Gang) and Piper\, Elias Alexander (The Bywater Band\, Soulsha\, MAC
 \, Fàrsan). Eamon Sefton accompanies them beautifully on guitar. Loud Wea
 ther delves deep into the essence of traditional tunes within an intricate
  framework\, with intertwining melodies\, soaring harmonies\, and vital rh
 ythms true to Scottish music’s danceable roots.

 through-mandolin/
SUMMARY:Windborne Midwinter Concert - Online through Mandolin
DESCRIPTION:Windborne is performing a full show of their favorite songs of 
 the season! A joyful and heartfelt celebration of song\, the lush vocals a
 nd unique arrangements of Lauren Breunig\, Jeremy Carter-Gordon\, Lynn Row
 an\, and Will Rowan bring light to a dark time\, as they draw on midwinter
  music from traditional\, classical\, and folk from the US\, British Isles
  and beyond. (This is not an SFS organized show\, but we are pleased to sh
 are the opportunity to enjoy Windborne before they come to Seattle in Marc
 h! A ticket to the online show allows 48 hours of access)

SUMMARY:Kiki Valera y su Son Cubano
DESCRIPTION:Exciting Cuban musical tradition for listening and dancing: Wit
 h its Spanish and African origins\, call-and-response format\, and infecti
 ous percussion beats\, Son Cubano was a major influence on many other form
 s of music\, from mambo and salsa to jazz and cha-cha-chá. Kiki Valera is
  a multi-instrumentalist\, composer\, arranger\, and virtuoso of the Cuban
  cuatro.  This is a seated concert\, but we will have an area set aside fo
 r dancing as well!

SUMMARY:Tall Poppy String Band
DESCRIPTION:A brand new Old Time trio featuring fiddler George Jackson\, gu
 itarist Morgan Harris\, and banjoist Cameron DeWhitt. Drawing from the dee
 p well of American string band music\, Tall Poppy String Band approaches t
 radition with playful curiosity\; each performance more a discussion than 
 a statement. Chasing the elusive downbeat of a source recording\, playing 
 with the pronouns in a bluegrass standard\, or challenging the assumed rol
 es of their instruments\, TPSB endeavours to prove that tradition is still
  being written.

SUMMARY:Miles & Karina Present "The Shakedown"
DESCRIPTION:David Miles Keenan and Nova Karina Devonie\, also known as Mile
 s & Karina\, perform one of their original scores to William Wyler's 1928 
 film\, "The Shakedown" about a pro boxer who intentionally loses matches. 
 The score was commissioned by the Frank Buxton Silent Film Festival. Dave 
 and Nova use guitar\, accordion\, toy piano\, hi-hat\, banjo\, glockenspie
 l and viola in this performance.

 tever else they decide to throw in the van -  Their concerts offer a disti
 nct blend of their musical inheritances and traditions as well as storytel
 ling about the ways that music and social movements coalesce. \n\nExciting
  news: Larry & Joe were just profiled as one of three featured artists in

 f-roan/
SUMMARY:Wollenberg\, Sturbaum\, & Hoequist / West of Roan
DESCRIPTION:Shared evening: West of Roan brings carefully crafted\, intuiti
 ve harmonies with voices and fiddle - from sweet to dissonant\, following 
 emotional arcs beneath the surface. Leah Wollenberg\, Ida Hoequist\, and A
 lex Sturbaum display a musical fluency and verve borne of long friendship 
 coupled with disparate influences. With tender waltzes\, mighty jigs and r
 eels\, and the odd full-throated labor song\, they have created a sound th
 at is both rooted in tradition and firmly of the moment.

URL:https://www.seafolklore.org/events/richard-shindell/
SUMMARY:Richard Shindell
DESCRIPTION:This show is sold out as of October 10th.\n\nRichard Shindell l
 ives as both an immigrant and emigrant\, crossing thresholds\, that inform
 s his illumination of the human experience through narrative song. Shindel
 l has inhabited a Zen Buddhist monastery and busked in the streets of Pari
 s. Originally from New York\, now living in Buenos Aires\, Argentina\, his
  songs paint pictures\, tell stories\, juxtapose ideas and images\, inhabi
 t characters\, vividly evoking entire worlds along the way and expanding o
 ur sense of a song can be.
ATTACH;FMTTYPE=image/jpeg:https://www.seafolklore.org/wp-content/uploads/2
 024/08/Richard-Shindell-by-Alejandro-Baccarat.jpeg

  folk and classical traditions of North India.  In this performance\, prem
 iering at Seattle Folklore Society\, she will perform songs from the deser
 ts of Rajasthan to the rolling hills of the North East\, and carry the lis
 teners on a melodic journey across North India.

URL:https://www.seafolklore.org/events/skye-consort-emma-bjorling-house-co
 ncert/
SUMMARY:Skye Consort & Emma Björling - House Concert
DESCRIPTION:Something for everyone! Seats for this concert are still availa
 ble! Skye Consort & Emma Björling perform trans-Atlantic arrangements of 
 songs and tunes from Sweden\, Norway\, Ireland\, Scotland\, England\, Qué
 bec\, Acadia\, the USA\, as well as original compositions. Each musician b
 rings their own individual interpretation to these traditions to create th
 is extraordinary collaboration\, consisting of voices\, fiddle\, nyckelhar
 pa\, cello\, bouzouki\, banjo\, and percussion. Collectively\, the band pr
 esents an assortment of whirling polskas\, groovy reels\, passionate love 
 songs\, breathtaking hymns\, and original compositions. 
ATTACH;FMTTYPE=image/jpeg:https://www.seafolklore.org/wp-content/uploads/2
 024/08/DSC00553_Skye-Consort-photo-scaled.jpg
CATEGORIES:Concerts
END:VEVENT
BEGIN:VEVENT
UID:273@seafolklore.org
DTSTART;TZID=America/Los_Angeles:20241110T190000
DTEND;TZID=America/Los_Angeles:20241110T213000
DTSTAMP:20240912T190730Z
URL:https://www.seafolklore.org/events/the-henry-girls/
SUMMARY:The Henry Girls
DESCRIPTION:The Henry Girls are a marvelous trio of three sisters from Mali
 n:  Karen\, Lorna and Joleen McLaughlin. They are great songwriters and h
 ave gorgeous\, super-tight three-part harmonies powered by the sisterhood 
 bond they share as well as their lengthy musical history.\n\nTwenty years 
 of gracing stages together\, this trio interweaves their native traditions
  with bluegrass\, jazz and country\, and are one of Ireland’s top emergi
 ng folk acts. With the harp as their main accompaniment\, their shimmering
  harmonies weave a continuous spell throughout their music. They have beco
 me one of the most talked about folk/roots acts to come out of Ireland\, n
 ow captivating audiences on worldwide stages having toured in Germany\, Am
 erica\, Sweden\, Italy\, Switzerland\, Holland\, Austria and the UK.\n\n\n
 \nTicket Type\n\nAdvance\n\n\nAt Door\n\n\n\nIn Person-General\n\n$24\n\n\

URL:https://www.seafolklore.org/events/lenajonsson-and-ian-carr/
SUMMARY:Lena Jonsson & Ian Carr - House Concert
DESCRIPTION:Lena Jonsson’s ability to balance a deep knowledge of traditi
 onal Swedish folk music with innovative artistic sensibilities\, sparkling
  joy of life and the charisma of a rock-star have made her one of Scandina
 via’s most visionary musicians. She has created a unique style inspired 
 by traditional Swedish music with rock\, pop and American old-time and blu
 egrass traditions. Together with guitarist Ian Carr\, you’ll be treated 
 to a virtuosic and playful evening of wonderful music.
ATTACH;FMTTYPE=image/jpeg:https://www.seafolklore.org/wp-content/uploads/2
 024/08/LenaJonsson_IanCarr.jpg

URL:https://www.seafolklore.org/events/dunava/
SUMMARY:Dunava
DESCRIPTION:We are delighted to feature the beautiful harmonies of Dunava o
 n our stage for a special Winter program! Named for the Bulgarian name for
  the Danube river\, Dunava is a Balkan women's choir\, made up of singers 
 with broad musical and performance experience and a passion for intricate 
 harmonies and varying vocal technique. Please note – by performer reques
 t all attendees will be required to mask while in the Hall for this concer
 t.

URL:https://www.seafolklore.org/events/newberry-and-verch/
SUMMARY:Newberry & Verch
DESCRIPTION:Growing up\, Joe Newberry and April Verch absorbed traditions o
 f home and hearth – in his Missouri Ozarks and her Ottawa Valley of Cana
 da. Although they are on the road much of the year\, the two musicians are
  fond of saying that they are rarely homesick\, because their music means 
 they always have a bit of home with them wherever they go.

 o-the-royal-room-co-sponsored-with-sfs/
SUMMARY:Italy's Ensemble Sangineto Returns to the Royal Room\, co-sponsored
  with SFS
DESCRIPTION:Ensemble Sangineto is an eclectic band which skillfully blends 
 ancient harmonies and sonorities with modern rhythms. The trio creates fre
 sh and new arrangements of traditional Irish\, Scottish\, Breton\, Italian
 \, and original compositions that display influences from varied musical g
 enres ranging from classical to folk\, Gregorian chant to musicals\, from 
 pop to Celtic music. Carnegie Hall’s head artistic director called them 
 “a unique ensemble indeed"!

SUMMARY:The Tune Tourists\, with opening set by Del Rey
DESCRIPTION:WB Reid\, Bonnie Zahnow\, and Ruthie Dornfeld are Seattle music
 ians who love to travel and have picked up fiddle tunes and songs from all
  around the Americas and Europe - From a French waltz to a Danish hopsa\, 
 Quebecois reels to old-time southern hoedowns\, Cajun 2-steps\, and dance 
 tunes from Michoacán\,  top notch twin fiddling and fine guitar and banjo
  accompaniment\, along with vocals from the Western swing\, Cajun\, Mexica
 n and blues traditions!

SUMMARY:Open the Door for Three
DESCRIPTION:Open the Door for Three is fiddle player Liz Knowles\, uillea
 nn piper Kieran O’Hare\, and Dublin-born singer and bouzouki player Pat 
 Broaders. Their music is a rare combination of unearthed tunes from centur
 ies-old collections\, newly composed melodies\, fresh arrangements of son
 gs old and new\, homages to the musicians and bands they grew up listening
  to\, and the unmatched energy of a trio of good friends playing great Iri
 sh music together.

SUMMARY:Scott Cook & Pamela Mae
DESCRIPTION:Scott's songs have won well-deserved acclaim from folk radio li
 sts and top honors in songwriting competitions. With his sweetheart Pamela
  Mae on upright bass\, banjo and vocals\, the pair has visited 45 states a
 nd 8 provinces in recent years while broadcasting solar-powered livestream
 s from the back of their campervan Roadetta. In 2025 they're releasing a n
 ew album called Troubadourly Yours. Fresh from the open road\, these are 
 sturdy\, straight-talking songs that see the good in you. \n

DESCRIPTION:Vintage jug band party  blues\, rags\, that sounds like they ar
 e lifted right out of 1928 in Memphis\, Tennessee! Complex and soulful voc
 al arrangements create rich and funky harmonies\, and a great feeling of 
 camaraderie\; the exuberance and fondness that the musicians feel for thei
 r material and for each other is unmistakable. with Suzy Thompson (fiddle\
 , vocals)\, Eric Thompson (mandolin)\, Frannie Leopold (vocals\, guitar)\,
  Bruce "WB" Reid (6-string banjo guitar\, vocals\,) and Matt Weiner (bass)

URL:https://www.seafolklore.org/events/scottish-fish/
SUMMARY:Scottish Fish
DESCRIPTION:\nScottish Fish present a fresh take on traditional and contemp
 orary Scottish and Cape Breton music. Their lively and unique arrangements
  are woven together from session music handed down from generations of the
  tradition’s finest players\, many of whom they have had the opportunity
  to share the stage with. These five young women have a very long and uniq
 ue history: they began the band as elementary school children\, and have r
 emained together for the last decade. Now in their late teens and early tw
 enties\, their intense chemistry is clear in their performances and music.
  You won't be able to keep your toes from tapping or hands from clapping\,
  especially when experiencing their arrangements live.\n\nScottish Fish ar
 e: Ava Montesi (fiddle)\, Caroline Dressler (fiddle)\, Giulia Haible (cell
 o\, piano)\, Julia Homa (fiddle\, piano)\, and Maggie MacPhail (fiddle\, p
 iano).\n\n[embed]https://youtu.be/G8ZFu_Gdbxs[/embed]\n\n[embed]https://yo
 utu.be/A8lee_hFBM0[/embed]\n\n\n\nTicket Type\n\nAdvance\n\n\nAt Door\n\n\

 th (6-17)\n\n$10\n\n\n$10\n\n\n\n\nBUY TICKETS HERE

SUMMARY:Muriel Anderson
DESCRIPTION:Muriel Anderson is considered a virtuoso of the 20-string harp-
 guitar\, yet it is her uplifting songs and melodies that leave her audienc
 e smiling. She is the first woman to win the National Fingerstyle Guitar C
 hampionship. Guitar Player Magazine lists her as one of the top 50 guitari
 sts of all time. Muriel’s multimedia show brings you on a planetary tour
  of tastes\, images\, and music with a backdrop of stunning visuals by pho
 to-artist Bryan Allen. \n

DESCRIPTION:Irish Fiddler Kevin Burke has been at the forefront of traditio
 nal music since the  1970’s. A founding member of several renowned and 
 influential groups (Open House\, Patrick Street\, The Celtic Fiddle Festiv
 al) Kevin has nonetheless retained his love for solo fiddle music – the 
 “naked fiddle” as he himself sometimes puts it. SFS is thrilled to wel

  Irish fiddle!

 ther Tommy on uilleann pipes\, fiddle\, and guitar.\n

URL:https://www.seafolklore.org/events/celtic-guitar-master-tony-mcmanus-a
 t-royal-room/
SUMMARY:Celtic Guitar Master Tony McManus at Royal Room
DESCRIPTION:Hailed by John Renbourn as 'the best Celtic guitarist in the wo
 rld\,’ Tony McManus has also been listed as one of the 50 transcendent g
 uitarists of all time by Guitar Player Magazine. Tony has both extended an
 d transcended the parameters of contemporary Celtic music. Ranked by peers
  and predecessors alike alongside the guitar world’s all-time greats\, h
 is fiendishly dexterous\, dazzlingly original playing draws on traditions 
 from the entire Celtic world\, along with further ranging flavours such as
  jazz and eastern European music. Long applauded for his uncanny ability t
 o transpose the delicate\, complex ornamentation characteristic of traditi
 onal bagpipe or fiddle tunes – even the phrasing of a Gaelic song – on
 to his own six strings\, McManus is being increasingly acknowledged as a p
 ioneering figure in bridging the realms of Celtic music and other guitar g
 enres\, performing with the likes of Alasdair Fraser\, Kevin Burke\, and N
 atalie McMaster\, among others. He has come to represent Celtic music in t
 he guitar world\, making appearances at guitar specific festivals such as 
 the Chet Atkins Festival in Nashville\; the Ellnora Guitar Festival in the
  Krannert Center\, and he has appeared at Guitar Festivals in Italy\, Aust
 ralia\, France\, Scotland\, England\, and Germany. He played at the famous
  Ryman Auditorium in Nashville in the “All Star Guitar Night” with Bé
 la Fleck and more\, headlined by the legendary Les Paul. His ability to re
 ach audiences unfamiliar with traditional music is also amazing\, even rea
 ching into classical events such as the Dundee and Derry Guitar festivals\
 , appearing six times between the two!\n\nTony will be appearing May 18 at
  the Royal Room\, a co-sponsorship with the Seattle Folklore Society\, tic
 kets are $25 advance\, $30 at the door\, and SFS members get a $5 discount
 !
ATTACH;FMTTYPE=image/jpeg:https://www.seafolklore.org/wp-content/uploads/2
 025/01/2022-08-19-Tony_McManus-e1738115191803.jpg
CATEGORIES:Concerts,Folk Calendar
LOCATION:The Royal Room\, 5000 Rainier Ave South\, Seattle\, WA\, United St
 ates
X-APPLE-STRUCTURED-LOCATION;VALUE=URI;X-ADDRESS=5000 Rainier Ave South\, Se
 attle\, WA\, United States;X-APPLE-RADIUS=100;X-TITLE=The Royal Room:geo:0
 ,0
END:VEVENT

URL:https://www.seafolklore.org/events/the-neon-brass-party-band-agab-free
 -outdoor-concert/
SUMMARY:The Neon Brass Party Band & AGAB - Free Outdoor Concert
DESCRIPTION:SFS SPONSORS A FREE AFTERNOON OUTDOOR CONCERT with the NEON BRA
 SS PARTY BAND and AGAB (Assigned Gay At Band) to cap off the PhinneyWood R
 ainbow Hop and Phinney Center Pride Party! Plan to join us from 1:30 to 2:
 30 at the Phinney Center upper lot for a closing celebration with room to 
 dance! The Neon Brass Party Band believes in creating space in the world t
 o celebrate our individuality together\, and finds its groove in styles fr
 om Latin to funk and hip-hop. AGAB is “a queer joy rebellion playing bra
 ss bangers for the gays and theys of every generation.” Both bands are f
 un\, exuberant and positive.\n\n\n\n\n\n&nbsp\;\n\n&nbsp\;\n\n&nbsp\;\n\n&
 nbsp\;\n\n&nbsp\;\n\nThe Rainbow Hop and Phinnney Center Activities start 
 at 10am\; all activities are listed at phinneycenter.org/events/Rainbow-ho
 p.\n\n\nThe PNA has a rich 40-year history of strengthening a sense of bel
 onging in our community\, which is illustrated by one of their signature e
 vents:  Rainbow Hop\, now in its 13th year!\n\nFamilies can travel the Ra
 inbow Hop route between 87th and 67th on Greenwood Avenue and take part in
  dozens of Pride-centric activities and crafts. Participating businesses a
 nd organizations will stamp the Rainbow Hop map as you participate through
 out the route. Kids with at least 6 stamps can exchange their maps for a f
 ree Woodland Park Zoo ticket in front of HomeStreet Bank between 12:30 and
  1:30pm (look for the guy in the rainbow cowboy hat). The Rainbow Hop map 
 and activity list is available as a pdf\, and copies can be picked up at t
 he Phinney Center Blue Building starting May 27th.\nThe 3rd Annual PNA Phi
 nney Center Pride Party celebration will be happening June 7th along with 
 Rainbow Hop!\n\nRainbow Hop along Greenwood | 10:30am - 1pm www.phinneycen
 ter.org/events/rainbow-hop/\n\nPNA Pride Party at the Phinney Center | 10:
 00am - 2:30pm\n10:00a- 1:30p Disco Dance with Sean aka “dj turtledove”
  - Room 3\n\n10:30a-11:15a Drag Queen Storytime with with Sylvia O'Stayfor
 more - Room 7\n\n11:30a-12:15p Belly Dancing class with Zaphara &amp\; liv
 e music with Sarah Comer's  Racoon Carnival band -  Room 2\n\n12:30a-1:1
 5p Drag Queen Storytime with with Sylvia O'Stayformore - Room 7\n\n1:30p-2
 :30p Afterparty!  SFS presents two exhuberant brass bands\, fresh from pe
 rformances at HonkFest West: The Neon Brass Party Band and AGAB (Assinged 
 Gay At Band) in the Upper Parking Lot  - Plan to join us at 1:30 for a cl
 osing celebration with room to dance!\n\nhttps://youtu.be/GvDZ-oeVAak?si=T
 4kt6DUPfYMsvxoj

 025/06/hfw-fundraiser-nbp_orig.jpg
CATEGORIES:Concerts,Folk Calendar
END:VEVENT

SUMMARY:Andy Irvine
DESCRIPTION:****This show is SOLD OUT****  (We are not able to maintain a w

 aturday noon**  Otherwise\, there may be a few ticketholders unable to att
 end at the last minute. If we are notified before the concert starts these
  will be available at the door when the hall opens at 7pm. We will not be 
 able to update any status after Saturday afternoon.) Andy Irvine is one of
  the great Irish singers\, his voice one of a handful of truly great ones 
 that gets to the very soul of Ireland. He has been hailed as "a tradition 
 in himself."  Musician\, singer and songwriter\, Andy has maintained his h
 ighly individual performing skills throughout his long career. "Often copi
 ed\, never equalled"\, his repertoire consists of Irish traditional songs\
 , dexterous Balkan dances and a compelling canon of his own self-penned so
 ngs.

 d showcase 2 local Scandinavian music bands for this concert. These young

 teel is a new project from hardanger fiddle player Mira Dickey and viola d

SUMMARY:Medusa Quartet
DESCRIPTION:Named after one of mythology’s most misunderstood figures\, t
 he Medusa Quartet channels the power of transformation\, reclaiming what w
 as once feared and turning it into something fiercely magical. Saskia Tomk
 ins – Violin\, Viola\, Cello\, Nyckelharpa\; Marta Solek – Suka\, Ploc
 k Fiddle\, Cello\;  Lea Kirstein – Violin\, Viola\, Cello\; Georgia Hath
 away – Violin\, Vocals -  A  genre-defying chamber-folk quartet that fus
 es centuries-old string traditions with contemporary storytelling and orig
 inal compositions.

URL:https://www.seafolklore.org/events/tret-fure/
SUMMARY:Tret Fure
DESCRIPTION:Tret Fure is an award-winning singer songwriter whose music dem
 onstrates a willingness to confront the most pressing issues of our day wi
 th courage\, compassion and a deep sense of humanity. Her songs speak to t
 he heart and the soul.  Besides the broad themes of the songs themselves 
 (addressing current social issues while also serving up some lovely love s
 ongs)\, her rich productions are creative with excellent guitar instrument
 ation and arrangements.

DESCRIPTION:After 25 years together\, Rani Arbo & daisy mayhem is an inde
 lible alchemy of song\, groove\, friendship and soul.  Beloved in their n
 ative New England\, they have toured across the U.S. and Canada to festiva
 ls\, theaters\, and coffeehouses\, leaving audiences humming\, hopeful\, a
 nd renewed "...some of the most soul-lifting songs you’ll hear in your l
 ife...pure magic happens when these four musicians play together... " Don
 ’t miss the chance to hear them on a rare West Coast tour!\n
